channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 4 takeaways from Tuesday's primary night in half a dozen states
item.description: While Trump continues to rack up victories on his vengeance tour, general-election opponents are waiting in swing districts and swing states. Can frontline GOP candidates navigate these choppy waters?
item.content:encoded:
While Trump continues to rack up victories on his vengeance tour, general-election opponents are waiting in swing districts and swing states. Can frontline GOP candidates navigate these choppy waters?

(Image credit: Brynn Anderson)
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: Overworked and understaffed: Special ed teachers turn to AI for help
item.description: A fast-growing number of special educators nationwide are using AI to create customized education plans. Despite the risks, some research shows it could improve the quality of teachers' work.
item.content:encoded:
A fast-growing number of special educators nationwide are using AI to create customized education plans. Despite the risks, some research shows it could improve the quality of teachers' work.

(Image credit: Talia Herman for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: Gun rights groups see 'golden age' under Trump administration
item.description: New gun rules and lawsuits against states signal how this Trump administration is using its powers to align itself with the wishes of the gun rights movement.
item.content:encoded:
New gun rules and lawsuits against states signal how this Trump administration is using its powers to align itself with the wishes of the gun rights movement.

(Image credit: Tyler Kaufman)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Bees have coexisted with us for over a millennia. Their name remains a mystery
item.description: Its name is short — like its size — but bees are one of Earth's most important and busy creatures.
item.content:encoded:
Its name is short — like its size — but bees are one of Earth's most important and busy creatures.

(Image credit: Alan Nakkash for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Denver has a plan to heat and cool buildings without fossil fuels. It involves … sewage?
item.description: Like many cities, Denver's largest source of greenhouse gas emissions is its buildings. Heating and cooling skyscrapers requires a lot of fossil fuels. Now, the city is trying a surprising solution.
item.content:encoded:
Like many cities, Denver's largest source of greenhouse gas emissions is its buildings. Heating and cooling skyscrapers requires a lot of fossil fuels. Now, the city is trying a surprising solution.

(Image credit: Aaron Ontiveroz)
item.dc:creator: Ishan Thakore

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: A look at primary results from around the country, Trump and Vance share conflicting messages on state of Iran war, shooting at San Diego mosque being investigated as possible "hate crime."
item.content:encoded: A look at primary results from around the country, Trump and Vance share conflicting messages on state of Iran war, shooting at San Diego mosque being investigated as possible "hate crime."
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Montana could soon ease restrictions on mining precious metals
item.description: Restrictions on mining for precious metals like gold in Montana that were put in place 30 years ago could soon be relaxed as the mining industry makes a comeback in the U.S.
item.content:encoded: Restrictions on mining for precious metals like gold in Montana that were put in place 30 years ago could soon be relaxed as the mining industry makes a comeback in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellis Juhlin

item.title: Shirtless fans cheer at MLB stadiums as "Tarps Off" trend sweeps baseball world
item.description: At ballparks all across the country, groups consisting of mostly young men are joining in on the trend: Go to the section where the party is happening, take off your shirt and start twirling it above your head.
item.content:encoded:
At ballparks all across the country, groups consisting of mostly young men are joining in on the trend: Go to the section where the party is happening, take off your shirt and start twirling it above your head.

(Image credit: Chris O'Meara)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Bolivia's capital under siege as protests deepen crisis for President Paz
item.description: Bolivia's President Rodrigo Paz faces a deepening crisis as widespread protests and blockades leave the political capital under siege less than six months after he took office.
item.content:encoded:
Bolivia's President Rodrigo Paz faces a deepening crisis as widespread protests and blockades leave the political capital under siege less than six months after he took office.

(Image credit: Freddy Barragan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: WHO chief concerned over 'scale and speed' of Ebola outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization top official has expressed concern over the rapid spread of a rare type of Ebola in Congo. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected deaths and over 500 cases.
item.content:encoded:
The World Health Organization top official has expressed concern over the rapid spread of a rare type of Ebola in Congo. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected deaths and over 500 cases.

(Image credit: Dirole Lotsima Dieudonne)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
